page: Microsoft Azure E4d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting hyper-threading with 2 threads per core. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 154 GB of local SSD storage, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1895.98 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3877.18 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3498.0. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, measuring 28.99 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 25.08 GB/sec for write operations. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform at average levels, with a Passmark encryption score of 3650.29 and static web serving throughput reaching 6.55 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the E4d_v5 offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for memory-intensive workloads that do not require heavy parallel CPU processing. The inclusion of 154 GB of local storage provides a fast scratch space, though the weak multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth represent clear tradeoffs for compute-heavy tasks. This server is best suited for memory-optimized workloads such as databases, caching layers, text processing, and basic web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E4d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0, alongside average performance in text processing and static web serving. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by a multi-core stress-ng score of 3877.18. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications rather than compute-intensive tasks. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and general memory-intensive workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local SSD storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and integrated local SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
154 GB of bundled local SSD storage
Average single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling
Well-suited for databases, caching, and memory-bound applications
